content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :   Memo to File                                      Date  :  May 26, 1976


From  :   FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
           Gina Siciliano Kutchins

Subject:


         On May 3, 1976 I spoke to Loretta Tulley, employed by the
law firm Green & Azevedo. She asked whether it was possible
under the Political Reform Act for registered state lobbyist to contribute to an elected state official who is running for a federal office. I stated that it was all right
if it was clear that the contribution was for the federal
candidacy. This information is not correct based on Sections
86200 and 86202 of the Political Reform Act.

         I called Ms. Tulley today, May 25, 1976, and informed her
that the original advice she received was incorrect. She
indicated that Don Green, the person for whom she requested
the information, had not acted on the advice and that to her
knowledge, based on the advice, no contribution by a lobbyist was made to a state candidate.

sections.question: Whether it is possible under the Political Reform Act for a registered state lobbyist to contribute to an elected state official who is running for federal office.
sections.conclusion: This information is not correct based on Sections 86200 and 86202 of the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: May a registered state lobbyist contribute to an elected state official running for federal office under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the original advice given was incorrect and that registered state lobbyists cannot make such contributions under Sections 86200 and 86202 of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal FPPC memo correcting erroneous advice previously given. No formal Q&A sections present. Document serves as both internal record and correction notice. The synthetic conclusion reflects the stated correction rather than a detailed legal analysis.

CONCLUSION: This information is not correct based on Sections 86200 and 86202 of the Political Reform Act.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : Memo to File Date : May 26, 1976 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Gina Siciliano Kutchins Subject: On May 3, 1976 I spoke to Loretta Tulley, employed by the law firm Green & Azevedo. She asked whether it was possible under the Political Reform Act for registered state lobbyist to contribute to an elected state official who is running for a federal office. I stated that it was all right if it was clear that the contribution was for the federal candidacy. This information is not correct based on Sections 86200 and 86202 of the Political Reform Act. I called Ms. Tulley today, May 25, 1976, and informed her that the original advice she received was incorrect. She indicated that Don Green, the person for whom she requested the information, had not acted on the advice and that to her knowledge, based on the advice, no contribution by a lobbyist was made to a state candidate.
embedding.summary: An internal FPPC memorandum documenting that incorrect advice was provided to a lobbyist regarding contributions to a state official running for federal office, and that the error was corrected before any contribution was made.
